front_text: Gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) refers to a spectrum of disorders arising from abnormal proliferation of placental trophoblastic tissue, encompassing both benign forms such as molar pregnancies and malignant forms such as invasive moles and choriocarcinoma.
front_html: Gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) refers to a spectrum of disorders arising from abnormal proliferation of {{c1::placental trophoblastic}} tissue, encompassing both benign forms such as molar pregnancies and malignant forms such as invasive moles and choriocarcinoma.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: A hallmark of gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) is markedly elevated beta-hCG levels, often much higher than expected for gestational age.
front_html: A hallmark of gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) is markedly elevated {{c1::beta-hCG}} levels, often much higher than expected for gestational age.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), excessive hCG stimulates the thyroid because hCG shares structural similarity with TSH, leading to hyperthyroid symptoms.
front_html: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), excessive hCG stimulates the thyroid because hCG shares structural similarity with {{c1::TSH}}, leading to hyperthyroid symptoms.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), elevated hCG causes exaggerated pregnancy symptoms including severe nausea and vomiting, (hyperemesis gravidarum), and a uterus larger than expected for gestational age.
front_html: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), elevated hCG causes exaggerated pregnancy symptoms including severe nausea and vomiting, ({{c1::hyperemesis gravidarum}}), and a uterus larger than expected for gestational age.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Risk factor for gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD): Maternal age extremes, specifically younger than 20 years or older than 35 years.
front_html: Risk factor for gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD): Maternal age extremes, specifically younger than {{c1::20}} years or older than {{c1::35}} years.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other risk factors for GTD:
• Previous molar pregnancy (highest risk for recurrence)
• history of miscarriage
• Asian ethnicity.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Risk factor for gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD): Previous molar pregnancy is the highest risk factor for recurrence.

front_html: Risk factor for gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD): History of {{c1::miscarriage}} is associated with increased GTD risk.

front_text: A complete mole occurs when an "empty" ovum with no maternal DNA is fertilized by one sperm that duplicates its chromosomes or by two sperm, resulting in 46,XX or 46,XY paternal-only genetic material.
front_html: A complete mole occurs when an "empty" ovum with no maternal DNA is fertilized by one sperm that duplicates its chromosomes or by two sperm, resulting in {{c1::46,XX}} or {{c1::46,XY}} paternal-only genetic material.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Complete mole feature: No fetal tissue is present.
front_html: Complete mole feature: {{c1::No}} fetal tissue is present.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other complete mole features:
• Diffuse swelling of chorionic villi giving a "grape-like" or "snowstorm" appearance
• markedly elevated hCG (often greater than 100,000 mIU/mL)
• uterus larger than expected for gestational age
• vaginal bleeding, positive pregnancy test, missed periods
• may present with early-onset preeclampsia (before 20 weeks) or hyperthyroid symptoms due to hCG cross-reactivity.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Complete mole feature: Diffuse swelling of chorionic villi produces a "grape-like" or "snowstorm" appearance.

front_text: An incomplete mole occurs when a normal ovum is fertilized by two sperm, leading to triploidy with karyotypes of 69,XXY, 69,XXX, or 69,XYY.
front_html: An incomplete mole occurs when a normal ovum is fertilized by two sperm, leading to {{c1::triploidy}} with karyotypes of {{c1::69,XXY}}, {{c1::69,XXX}}, or {{c1::69,XYY}}.

curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Incomplete mole feature: Fetal or embryonic tissue may be present.
front_html: Incomplete mole feature: {{c1::Fetal}} or {{c1::embryonic}} tissue may be present.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other incomplete mole features:
• Mixture of normal and abnormal villi
• moderately elevated hCG, but lower than in complete mole
• often presents as a missed or incomplete abortion with vaginal bleeding
• less likely to cause preeclampsia or hyperthyroid symptoms.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: On ultrasound, a complete mole shows a "snowstorm" or "cluster of grapes" pattern due to swollen, cystic villi and absence of fetal tissue.
front_html: On ultrasound, a complete mole shows a {{c1::"snowstorm"}} or {{c1::"cluster of grapes"}} pattern due to swollen, cystic villi and absence of fetal tissue.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other GTD ultrasound findings:
• Incomplete mole may show gestational sac and fetal parts, often with oligohydramnios
• theca lutein cysts on ovaries due to high hCG stimulation (resolve after evacuation).
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: On ultrasound, an incomplete mole may show a gestational sac and fetal parts, often with oligohydramnios.
front_html: On ultrasound, an incomplete mole may show a gestational sac and fetal parts, often with {{c1::oligohydramnios}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other GTD ultrasound findings:
• Complete mole shows "snowstorm" or "cluster of grapes" pattern due to swollen, cystic villi and absence of fetal tissue
• theca lutein cysts on ovaries due to high hCG stimulation (resolve after evacuation).
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), theca lutein cysts form on the ovaries due to high hCG stimulation and resolve after uterine evacuation.
front_html: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), {{c1::theca lutein cysts}} form on the ovaries due to high hCG stimulation and resolve after uterine evacuation.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: An invasive mole develops from a previously benign molar pregnancy and invades the myometrium, leading to uterine bleeding or perforation.
front_html: An invasive mole develops from a previously benign molar pregnancy and invades the {{c1::myometrium}}, leading to uterine bleeding or perforation.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: On ultrasound, an invasive mole appears as anechoic (dark) areas with high vascular flow.
front_html: On ultrasound, an invasive mole appears as {{c1::anechoic (dark)}} areas with high vascular flow.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: An invasive mole can metastasize but remains highly responsive to chemotherapy.
front_html: An invasive mole can metastasize but remains highly responsive to {{c1::chemotherapy}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma is a highly malignant trophoblastic cancer that can arise after a molar pregnancy, a normal term pregnancy, an abortion, or an ectopic pregnancy.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma is a highly malignant trophoblastic cancer that can arise after a {{c1::molar pregnancy}}, a normal term pregnancy, an abortion, or an ectopic pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma contains no chorionic villi, only abnormal trophoblast proliferation with areas of necrosis and hemorrhage.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma contains {{c1::no chorionic villi}}, only abnormal trophoblast proliferation with areas of {{c1::necrosis}} and {{c1::hemorrhage}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma produces early metastases to the lungs, brain, and liver.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma produces early metastases to the {{c1::lungs}}, {{c1::brain}}, and {{c1::liver}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 1 is confined to the uterus.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 1 is confined to the {{c1::uterus}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other choriocarcinoma stages:
• Stage 2: involved genital structures
• Stage 3: lung metastasis
• Stage IV: metastasis to distant organs.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 2 involves genital structures.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 2 involves {{c1::genital structures}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other choriocarcinoma stages:
• Stage 1: confined to uterus
• Stage 3: lung metastasis
• Stage IV: metastasis to distant organs.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 3 involves lung metastasis.
front_html: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage 3 involves {{c1::lung metastasis}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other choriocarcinoma stages:
• Stage 1: confined to uterus
• Stage 2: involved genital structures
• Stage IV: metastasis to distant organs.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Choriocarcinoma staging: Stage IV involves metastasis to distant organs.

curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Treatment of molar pregnancy (complete or incomplete): The preferred method of uterine evacuation is suction curettage, followed by histologic examination to confirm diagnosis.
front_html: Treatment of molar pregnancy (complete or incomplete): The preferred method of uterine evacuation is {{c1::suction curettage}}, followed by histologic examination to confirm diagnosis.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Molar pregnancy follow-up: Serum hCG is measured weekly until undetectable for 3 consecutive weeks, then monthly for 6 months while using reliable contraception to avoid confusion with a new pregnancy.
front_html: Molar pregnancy follow-up: Serum hCG is measured weekly until undetectable for {{c1::3 consecutive}} weeks, then monthly for {{c1::6}} months while using reliable contraception to avoid confusion with a new pregnancy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment, low-risk (score ≤6): Single-agent methotrexate therapy.
front_html: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment, low-risk (score {{c1::≤6}}): Single-agent {{c1::methotrexate}} therapy.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other persistent GTD treatment:
• High-risk (score greater than 6): Combination chemotherapy with EMA-CO regimen (Etoposide, Methotrexate, Actinomycin D, Cyclophosphamide, Vincristine)
• surgery (hysterectomy) for localized resistant disease.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment, high-risk (score >6): Combination chemotherapy with the EMA-CO regimen, consisting of Etoposide, Methotrexate, Actinomycin D, Cyclophosphamide, and Vincristine.
front_html: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment, high-risk (score {{c1::>6}}): Combination chemotherapy with the {{c1::EMA-CO}} regimen, consisting of {{c1::Etoposide}}, {{c1::Methotrexate}}, {{c1::Actinomycin D}}, {{c1::Cyclophosphamide}}, and {{c1::Vincristine}}.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra: Other persistent (GTD) treatment elements: Low-risk (score ≤6): Single-agent methotrexate therapy. Surgery (hysterectomy) may be used for localized resistant disease. Remission: Defined by 3 consecutive undetectable hCG results during weekly testing.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment: Surgery (hysterectomy) may be used for localized resistant disease.
front_html: Persistent gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD) treatment: Surgery ({{c1::hysterectomy}}) may be used for localized resistant disease.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
extra:
Other persistent GTD treatment:
• Low-risk (score ≤6): Single-agent methotrexate therapy
• High-risk (score greater than 6): EMA-CO combination chemotherapy.
curriculum_topic_path: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health > Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20% > Gestational trophoblastic disease

front_text: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), remission is defined by 3 consecutive undetectable hCG results during weekly testing.
front_html: In gestational trophoblastic disease (GTD), remission is defined by {{c1::3 consecutive}} undetectable hCG results during weekly testing.
tags: Gynecologic, Sexual, and Reproductive Health,Prenatal Care/Pregnancy – 20%,Gestational trophoblastic disease
